reasoning: The 180-day average SOFR is mechanically determined by the daily SOFR rates dropping out of the 180-day window and new daily rates entering it. For the average to increase, the new rates must be higher than the rates exiting the window. The rates exiting the 180-day window over the next month (August/September 2025) are around 4.1-4.3%, while current daily SOFR is much lower at around 3.66%. Barring an unprecedented multi-day spike in repo rates (which the Fed's Standing Repo Facility is designed to prevent), the mathematical reality guarantees the 180-day average will continue its steady daily decline. Therefore, the chance of the average increasing by February 22 or March 17 is vanishingly small.
